text: Can a biblical doctrine of God have a Greek inheritance? The article explores the origins and components of the Orthodox formula of the Trinity in an attempt to decide the extent to which the Nicene understanding of God is coloured by Greek philosophy and so contrasts traditional Hellenistic Trinitarian formulations with the biblical revelation of God as Trinity. With close engagement of the current discussion in dogmatics, the article assesses whether the Trinitarian formula should be regarded as a mystery rather than a dogmatic formula and hence needs “dehellenization.” This means that the terms and concepts used by the Church Fathers and after Nicaea were employed not for explanatory reasons but merely as a contextualization tool using doxological and liturgical terms, some of which can be traced back to Scripture.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
